item.title: In Hungary, Orbánomics is on the ballot
item.description: The country’s economic model has stoked inflation and weighed on growth

item.title: How to make shoplifting a crime with consequences
item.description: Police, shop owners and policymakers must combine to fight organised retail theft

item.title: Social media is for picking up vibes, not information
item.description: Users trust it even less than they do mainstream news outlets

item.title: A giant leap for jargon
item.description: The latest Nasa voyage shows the pitfalls of insider language afflict even the smartest people on the planet

item.title: How will AI change the org chart?
item.description: At the micro level, there is evidence that it pays to collapse the layers of command

item.title: The S&P 500’s woes in Trump 2.0
item.description: The index has had its worst performance over the past five US presidencies

item.title: Only two events have mattered in my 30 years of investing
item.description: Markets have shrugged off everything from geopolitics to financial crises

item.title: Jacob’s Creek should come in smaller bottles
item.description: Relaunch of popular 1990s tipple might need more than nostalgia to stand out from today’s crowd

item.title: Royal balm will not rescue the Anglo-American relationship
item.description: The King’s upcoming visit to the court of Donald Trump has too much riding on it

item.title: BP’s proxy fight exposes bugs in shareholder voting machine
item.description: On contentious matters, investors are less likely to follow proxies

item.title: Yet more reasons to look on the bright side . . .
item.description: The science suggests that it is healthier to be wrong and happy than to be pessimistic and right

item.title: The tragedy of leisure
item.description: The combination of AI and small families will create more free time than is good for us

item.title: Against all odds, American hospitality prevails
item.description: A recent visit reminded me why LA is (still) one of the best food cities in the world

item.title: Separating the signal from the noise in private credit
item.description: What matters most is how capital was deployed and underwritten

item.title: How Streep and Wintour dealt with the Devil — and won
item.description: A long-awaited sequel tells us that this is the season of the septuagenarian

item.title: Jancis Robinson’s A-Z of 21st-century wine — part one
item.description: From Azores to Influencers: the first of three guides to what’s new in the wine world

item.title: The invitations we consider in our lives
item.description: Stepping into the unknown can take us towards new versions of ourselves. But for this to happen we need to say yes

item.title: Can design make people feel at home?
item.description: Safe and intimate, cosy and comforting: the feeling may be intangible, but you know it when it’s right

item.title: Trump is surrendering America’s moral leadership
item.description: The president’s chilling threats have further eroded rules against war crimes

item.title: European stocks are the losers in the Iran war fallout
item.description: The US market is more immune from shocks given still healthy American economic conditions and earnings

item.title: Trumpism has done remarkably little to the GOP’s centre of gravity
item.description: Most of the things that make it distinct from traditional conservatism won’t last

item.title: Not scaring investors off is only the start
item.description: UK officials are concerned that standard financial health warnings such as ‘Your capital is at risk’ are driving savers away

item.title: Why I will always love Canary Wharf
item.description: The business district in London’s Docklands demonstrates what innovation and invention can achieve

item.title: How can I sound more presidential?
item.description: Forget strong, silent leadership. These days you’re going to need to swear. Bigly

item.title: The NHS isn’t getting better
item.description: Tough medicine is needed to resolve rolling strikes
